incidents.title: Checkout 5xx after connection pool config change
incidents.root_cause: A pool-size reduction deployed without canary exhausted available connections under normal peak load in smaller regions. US regions were unaffected because their pools had headroom.
incidents.resolution: Rolled back the config change. Error rate recovered within 6 minutes.
incidents.action_items_status: 2 of 3 completed. 'Add canary stage to config deploy pipeline' remains OPEN.
incidents.note: Same shape as a config-only change reaching all regions at once.

incidents.title: Anomalous single-ASN traffic to quote endpoint
incidents.root_cause: A partner's mobile SDK shipped with an aggressive retry loop and no jitter. Traffic was legitimate but badly behaved. No customer impact — quote endpoint absorbed it.
incidents.resolution: Contacted partner, they shipped a fix in 9 days. Added per-ASN rate limiting to the backlog.
incidents.action_items_status: 1 of 2 completed. 'Per-ASN rate limiting at gateway' remains OPEN.
incidents.note: Traffic signature was near-identical to a scraping attack. Initial verdict was ATTACK; revised after partner contact.

incidents.title: Total checkout outage — expired PSP certificate
incidents.root_cause: PSP client certificate expired. No expiry monitoring existed.
incidents.action_items_status: All 3 completed. Certificate expiry monitoring now in place.
incidents.note: Failure was total, instant and region-uniform — the opposite signature to a partial regional degradation.
